content.problemStatement:
Let f(X)f(X)f(X) denote the number of set bits in XXX.

Given integers NNN and KKK, construct an array AAA of length NNN such that:


0≤Ai≤K0 \leq A_i \leq K0≤Ai​≤K for all (1≤i≤N)(1 \le i \le N)(1≤i≤N);
∑i=1NAi=K\sum_{i=1}^{N} A_i = K∑i=1N​Ai​=K
Over all such possible arrays, find the **maximum** value of ∑i=1Nf(Ai)\sum_{i=1}^{N} f(A_i)∑i=1N​f(Ai​).


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follow.
The only line of each test case contains integers NNN and KKK- the length of the array and sum of elements respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output the **maximum** value of ∑i=1Nf(Ai)\sum_{i=1}^{N} f(A_i)∑i=1N​f(Ai​).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Only one array is possible that is A=[7]A =[7]A=[7]. As 7=(111)27 = (111)_27=(111)2​, the number of set bits in 777 is 333. Thus, ∑i=1Nf(Ai)=f(7)=3\sum_{i=1}^{N} f(A_i) = f(7) = 3∑i=1N​f(Ai​)=f(7)=3.
Test case 222: A possible array satisfying all the conditions is A=[3,0,3]A =[3, 0, 3]A=[3,0,3]. As 3=(11)23 = (11)_23=(11)2​, the number of set bits in 333 is 222. Similarly, the number of set bits in 000 is 000. Thus, ∑i=1Nf(Ai)=f(3)+f(0)+f(3)=4\sum_{i=1}^{N} f(A_i) = f(3) + f(0) + f(3) = 4∑i=1N​f(Ai​)=f(3)+f(0)+f(3)=4. It can be proven that answer never exceeds 444.
